title: Daily Briefing — May 25, 2026
headline: Flows are bullish, but breadth and sector risk are starting to flash yellow.
body: Right, so the big picture: The market’s grinding toward new highs—SPY up 0.4% at $745.64, well above its SMA20 ($731.58) and tech (XLK) leading, but VIX is chilling at 16.64, signaling low volatility. Not seeing panic, and overall risk conditions are normal, but we’re drifting into late-cycle territory with greed picking up (Fear & Greed now 58.6). Abundance mindset, yes—but don’t get complacent.

Sector rotation: XLK (tech) remains king with RS=118.2 and improving momentum. Meanwhile, Energy (XLE) is weakening, and Healthcare, Real Estate, Staples, Financials, and Utilities are ticking up in relative strength—classic signs of defensive sector improvement. XLRE triggered above all MAs, while XLF just reclaimed SMA20 at $51.94. Pay attention to Materials and Staples—rotation looks real here.

Contrarian/risk: Several tech names (AAPL, NVDA, ARM, DELL, XLK, SM, AA, COPX) are extended above their SMA20s. Retail looks to be chasing—record moves like DELL (+16.8%) and ARM (+2.8%) often attract frothy flows. Breadth isn’t breaking, but lagging sectors (Industrials, Communication) have elevated risk. Watch out for a shift if we see Smart Money confidence drop below 0.3 or defensive sector flows accelerate. Don’t chase, abundance mindset—keep your eye on the 20 MA, always.
callout_body:
SPY: $745.64 (SMA20 $731.58; above mean reversion zone)
QQQ: $717.54 (SMA20 $694.91)
XLK: $180.39 (SMA20 $170.56; leading)
XLE: $59.49 (SMA20 $58.48; weakening)
XLF: $51.94 (SMA20 $51.59; improving)
XLRE: $44.56 (SMA20 $44.16; triggered)
VIX: 16.64 (below SMA20 17.56)
HYG: $79.91 (SMA20 $79.87; healthy, but keep watching)
If daily close drops below 20 MA on any major index, exit longs (Rule R006)
Fear & Greed not extreme yet, but inching toward risk zone.
action_items: Watch tech (XLK, NVDA, ARM, DELL) for overextension—don’t chase; set stops at 20 MA.
Monitor sector rotation—Healthcare (XLV), Staples (XLP), Real Estate (XLRE), and Financials (XLF) for defensive plays if flows confirm.
closing_quote: Retail chasing a move? Be suspicious. Less trades = more wins.
